title: A replay-tangent update can reopen Q2.5's constrained path
body: Q2.5 showed that scalar continuation alone is insufficient: its terminal 1/128 candidates missed the 1.5% cumulative boundary by only 0.000151 to 0.000238 percentage points. Removing only the component of each AdamW weight displacement that points uphill on the pre-attempt mean six-slice replay surface may admit a direction that continues quantity learning without changing any data, gate, or evaluation authority.
claims: Q2.5 isolates update direction, rather than a weaker replay threshold or a smaller scalar step, as the next controlled variable.
The projection changes candidate construction only; direct six-slice functional evaluation remains the sole commit authority.
